description: Machine-readable workload taxonomy for insert_me evaluation-only C/C++ targets. Used by characterize_workloads.py and support_envelope.md.
classification_algorithm: Primary axis is LOC (total source lines). File count is secondary. Inspect suitability tier (corpus_generation, pilot_small_batch, pilot_single) is the target-side confirmation.

tiny.label: tiny
tiny.support_label: pilot only -- honest shortfall expected above recommended_max_count
tiny.primary_use: single-case manual experiments; not suitable for batch corpus generation
tiny.parallelization_benefit: negligible -- bottleneck is capacity, not throughput

small.label: small
small.support_label: supported -- pilot to small-corpus; mild shortfall possible above ~20
small.primary_use: small corpora 5-20 cases; multi-target portfolio starter
small.parallelization_benefit: low -- per-case time 150-200ms; sequential is fast enough

medium.label: medium
medium.support_label: recommended -- primary corpus generation target class
medium.primary_use: primary corpus generation 20-60 cases; portfolio multi-target with small targets

large_phase16.label: large (phase 16 projection)
large_phase16.support_label: projected only -- not validated in phase 16; expect validator bottleneck to dominate
large_phase16.primary_use: future phase; parallelization likely required before this class is practical
large_phase16.parallelization_benefit: high -- validator scales with source size; parallelism would be impactful

minimal.notes: Reference tiny target. 1-file concentration triggers honest shortfall above count=3.

demo.notes: Primary expert/manual demo fixture. 2 files, low capacity. Good for run --seed-file demos.

moderate.notes: Reference small target. Good first corpus target for new users.

target_b.notes: Compact but dense small target. Higher LOC/file than moderate.

sandbox_eval.notes: Primary medium reference target. All 6 admitted strategies viable. Recommended for corpus generation benchmarks.

stage_timing_benchmarks.note: Approximate median times from 5-run measurement on reference hardware. All values in milliseconds.

dominant_stage_by_class.tiny: auditor (schema validation overhead dominates at small file count)
dominant_stage_by_class.medium: validator (file I/O for tree comparison -- 60% of pipeline time)

stage_timing_benchmarks.bottleneck_finding: Validator (file I/O for bad/good tree comparison) is the dominant stage for small and medium targets, consuming 56-60% of per-case pipeline time. It scales with source tree size. Seeder scales with candidate pool size.

workflow_timing_benchmarks.note: Approximate wall-clock times including subprocess startup. Single run.
workflow_timing_benchmarks.planning_note: plan-corpus is O(count * strategies * candidates). For tiny targets with few candidates, planning can take longer than generation due to exhaustive sweep and honest shortfall resolution.
